agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial controls.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through states: trial, active, renewing, grace period, cancelled."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees. Has a validity/status (active, failed)."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring time period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when payment is collected."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging the subscriber's payment method to extend the subscription for another billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window. Up to 3 attempts are made for standard tiers."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A 14-day window granted to Premium tier subscribers after failed renewal, during which the subscription remains active before cancellation."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A time-limited subscription granted without payment, restricted to one active instance per email address."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives preferential treatment during renewal failures (grace period instead of 3-strike cancellation)."},{"term":"Cancelled Subscription","definition":"A terminal subscription state. Cannot be reactivated; the customer must create a new subscription."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription against a known-bad payment method would create an immediately corrupt state — the system would promise service it cannot collect revenue for. This is a precondition guard, not a policy preference.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with a failed payment method, WHEN they attempt to activate a subscription, THEN the activation is rejected and the subscription remains inactive."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy tuning parameter. A VP could adjust this to 5 or 10 days based on payment processor retry windows or business needs without corrupting system state. The concept of early renewal is important, but the specific window is policy.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an active subscription approaching its billing cycle end, WHEN the date is exactly 7 days before the cycle ends, THEN the system initiates an auto-renewal charge attempt."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The threshold of 3 attempts is a business policy decision balancing customer retention against revenue risk. Management could change this to 2 or 5 attempts. However, the principle that repeated failures eventually lead to cancellation is sound business logic — the specific count is policy, not a physical or data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an active subscription with 2 prior failed renewal attempts, WHEN a 3rd renewal attempt fails, THEN the subscription status transitions to cancelled."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. 'Cancelled' is a terminal state. Allowing reactivation of cancelled subscriptions would break audit trails, potentially resurrect stale pricing/terms, and corrupt the subscription lifecycle model. The domain enforces that cancelled is final — new business requires a new aggregate.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a subscription in cancelled state, WHEN any actor attempts to reactivate it, THEN the system rejects the request and the subscription remains cancelled."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day duration and the decision to use a grace period model instead of strike-counting for Premium are both business policy choices. These reflect a retention strategy for high-value customers and could be adjusted (e.g., 21 days, or switching Premium to 5 strikes) without violating data integrity.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal, WHEN the failure occurs, THEN the subscription enters a 14-day grace period instead of incrementing a failure counter, and is cancelled only if payment is not recovered within 14 days."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a uniqueness constraint protecting business integrity. Allowing multiple concurrent trials per email would enable abuse of the trial system, directly undermining the business model. This is analogous to a unique constraint in the data model — violating it corrupts the system's understanding of trial eligibility.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer email address that already has an active free trial subscription, WHEN that email attempts to create another free trial subscription, THEN the system rejects the request and no new trial is created."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose only payment method on file has a 'failed' status","when":"The customer attempts to activate a new subscription","then":"The system rejects activation with a clear error indicating the payment method must be resolved first"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending in exactly 7 days","when":"The system's scheduled renewal job runs","then":"A renewal charge is initiated against the subscriber's payment method"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription that has failed renewal 2 times already","when":"The 3rd renewal attempt also fails","then":"The subscription status transitions to 'cancelled' and the customer is notified"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' status","when":"Any API call or user action attempts to set it back to 'active'","then":"The system returns an error and the subscription remains cancelled; no state mutation occurs"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium tier subscription whose renewal has just failed","when":"The system evaluates the failure","then":"The subscription enters a grace period state with a 14-day expiration, and no strike counter is incremented"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A Premium tier subscription in grace period for 14 days without successful payment","when":"The grace period expires","then":"The subscription is automatically cancelled"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An email address with an active free trial subscription","when":"The same email attempts to start a second free trial","then":"The system rejects the request, preserving the existing trial unchanged"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An email address whose previous free trial has ended or been cancelled","when":"That email attempts to start a new free trial","then":"The system's behavior is undefined by current rules — escalation needed on whether lifetime trial limits apply"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer fixes their failed payment method during the renewal retry window","expected_behavior":"The next renewal attempt should succeed and the subscription should remain active, resetting any failure counter."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber pays during the 14-day grace period","expected_behavior":"Subscription should return to active status, grace period is cleared, and the billing cycle resets."},{"scenario":"Customer has multiple payment methods, one failed and one valid","expected_behavior":"BR-1 says 'a failed payment method on file' — ambiguous whether any failed method blocks activation or only if all methods are failed. Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Free trial user changes email address mid-trial and starts a new trial with old email","expected_behavior":"If the system tracks by email, the old email no longer has an active trial, so a new trial under the old email should be allowed. But this may constitute abuse."},{"scenario":"3rd renewal failure and grace period overlap for a subscriber who upgrades to Premium mid-renewal","expected_behavior":"Tier at time of failure evaluation determines which rule applies. If upgraded to Premium before cancellation triggers, grace period should apply."},{"scenario":"Subscription cancelled after 3 failures, but payment actually succeeded asynchronously","expected_behavior":"If the payment processor confirms success after cancellation, the system must decide whether to honor the terminal state (BR-4) or handle as a refund case. BR-4 says cancelled is terminal."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect activation with failed payments leads to revenue leakage. Overly aggressive cancellation (3-strike) risks losing recoverable subscribers. Grace period for Premium retains high-value customers but extends exposure to non-payment.","operational":"The 7-day renewal window creates a dependency on reliable job scheduling. Grace period tracking adds state management complexity. Trial uniqueness requires real-time email lookups at trial creation.","user":"Customers benefit from retry windows (not immediately cancelled on first failure). Premium customers get preferential treatment which reinforces tier value. Trial abuse prevention protects legitimate users but may frustrate users with multiple email-adjacent accounts.","risk":"Terminal cancellation state (BR-4) is irreversible — any bug that incorrectly cancels a subscription cannot be undone within the domain model, requiring out-of-band remediation. Grace period logic for Premium must be carefully tested to avoid accidentally applying the 3-strike rule.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Subscribers (Standard Tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike cancellation; need clear communication about retry status and remaining attempts."},{"group":"Subscribers (Premium Tier)","impact":"Receive preferential grace period; must understand their subscription won't be cancelled immediately but will be after 14 days."},{"group":"Finance/Revenue Team","impact":"Grace periods delay revenue recognition certainty. Trial abuse controls protect against free-tier exploitation."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Cannot reactivate cancelled subscriptions; must guide customers to create new ones. Will handle edge cases around payment timing and grace periods."},{"group":"Engineering/Platform","impact":"Must implement a state machine with terminal states, tier-aware failure handling, and uniqueness constraints on trials."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively clear and internally consistent. Rules are explicit with specific numbers. Confidence is docked because several edge cases remain unaddressed: lifetime trial limits, multi-payment-method behavior, and mid-cycle tier changes. No contradictions detected.","high_confidence_areas":["Terminal cancellation state is clearly defined","3-strike rule for standard tier is unambiguous","Trial uniqueness constraint per email is clear","7-day renewal window is explicit"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'failed payment method on file' means ANY failed method or the PRIMARY method — affects multi-payment-method scenarios","Whether free trial limits are per-email-per-lifetime or only concurrent","What happens to Premium grace period if subscriber is downgraded during the grace window","Whether the 7-day window means exactly 7 days or at-least 7 days before cycle end"],"assumptions":["Each subscription has a single primary payment method (not multiple)","Premium grace period and standard 3-strike rule are mutually exclusive — a subscriber is evaluated under one model based on their tier at time of failure","Free trial uniqueness is enforced only for concurrently active trials, not lifetime"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"BR-1 states 'a failed payment method on file' but does not clarify behavior when a customer has multiple payment methods — does one failed method among several valid ones block activation, or only when the designated/primary method is failed?","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Customer Support","Subscribers"],"options":["Block activation if ANY payment method on file is failed","Block activation only if the PRIMARY/default payment method is failed","Block activation only if ALL payment methods are failed"],"recommendation":"Option B — block only if the primary payment method is failed. This is standard industry practice and avoids punishing customers who have a stale secondary card on file."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Free trial uniqueness is defined as 'one active trial per email at any time' but does not address whether a customer who completed or cancelled a trial can start another one. 'At any time' could mean concurrent-only or lifetime.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Team","Finance","Subscribers"],"options":["One trial per email lifetime — once used, never again","One concurrent trial per email — after trial ends, a new one is allowed","One trial per email per product/plan — different plans allow separate trials"],"recommendation":"Clarify with product team. Industry standard is typically one trial per email lifetime to prevent abuse, but the current wording only prohibits concurrent trials."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
